DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS TOM O'CONNOR, KEVIN COYLE, MICHAEL SCHWALM'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE CONTEMPT AND MOTION FOR AN AWARD OF MONETARY AND TERMINATION SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANTS: THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE VIOLATED PRIOR COURT ORDERS COMPELLING FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY. DEFENDANTS ARE DIRECTED TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO THE DISCOVERY ARTICULATED IN THE MOTION NO LATER THAN FEBRUARY 11, 2022 AND PAY SANCTIONS OF $6,715.00 TO PLAINTIFFS. THE COURT SETS THIS MATTER FOR FURTHER HEARING ON MARCH 4, 2022 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPARTMENT 301 TO CONFIRM DEFENDANTS' COMPLIANCE WITH THIS ORDER. IF THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT FULLY COMPLIED, THE COURT INTENDS TO ISSUE TERMINATING OR ISSUE PRECLUSION SANCTIONS. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFFS IS DIRECTED TO PROVIDE BY FEBRUARY 28, 2022 A BRIEF UPDATE VIA EMAIL ON THE STATUS TO JUDGE PRO TEM MCDONALD. PLAINTIFF/PREVAILING PARTY TO SEND PROPOSED ORDER TRACKING THE ADOPTED RULING OF THE COURT VIA EMAIL TO DAVIDIDAHO14@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: DAVID MCDONALD, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

Compliance Hearing Re: Plaintiffs Tom O'Connor, Kevin Coyle, Michael Schwalm'S Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And Motion For An Award Of Monetary And Termination Sanctions Against Defendants.
Off Calendar Apr-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: (via Zoom Videoconference)
David Monroe, of Hanning Law LLP, Tel. 650-482-3040, appearing on behalf of the plaintiffs;
Jonathan Bornstein, of Bornstein & Bornstein Law Group, Tel. 510-901-0010, appearing on behalf of the defendants.

The matter was continued from a prior hearing. Both parties previously stipulated to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem.

RULING: The matter is discussed. Compliance Hearing Re: Plaintiffs Tom O'Connor, Kevin Coyle, Michael Schwalm's Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And Motion For An Award Of Monetary And Termination Sanctions Against Defendants is taken UNDER SUBMISSION.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Felicia Green; Not reported; Dept. 302.

Compliance Hearing Re: Plaintiffs Tom O'Connor, Kevin Coyle, Michael Schwalm'S Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And Motion For An Award Of Monetary And Termination Sanctions Against Defendants.
Off Calendar Mar-21-2022 Continued To Mar-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Attys. MICHAEL J. ELIZONDO, Esq. and DAVID L. MONROE, Esq. of Hannig Law LLP (Tel: 650-482-3040) for plaintiffs;
Atty. JONATHAN H. BORNSTEIN, Esq. of Bornstein & Bornstein Law Group (Tel: 510-901-0010) for defendants Laus Investment Group, Sophie Lau, Jeffrey Lau, and Gina Lau

Compliance Hearing Re: Plaintiffs Tom O'Connor, Kevin Coyle, Michael Schwalm'S Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And Motion For An Award Of Monetary And Termination Sanctions Against Defendants.

Court and counsel discuss the status of defendants' payment of monetary sanctions payment and production of discovery. Defense counsel represents that he mailed a check for payment of the monetary sanctions yesterday by overnight mail and that plaintiffs' counsel should be receive the check today. Defense counsel represents that defendants will produce verified responses without objections to all outstanding discovery on plaintiffs by March 7, 2022.

The Court takes the matter under submission while the completion of defendants' discovery production is pending. The Court invites plaintiffs' counsel to either file a brief or send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com by March 10, 2022 to provide the Court with an update on defendants' compliance/non-compliance.

Judge Pro Tem: David McDonald; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)

PLAINTIFFS TOM O'CONNOR, KEVIN COYLE, MICHAEL SCHWALM's Motion For An Order To Show Cause Re Contempt And Motion For An Award Of Monetary And Termination Sanctions Against Defendants:
The Court finds that Defendants have violated prior Court orders compelling further responses to discovery. Defendants are directed to provide further responses to the discovery articulated in the motion no later than February 11, 2022 and pay sanctions of $6,715.00 to Plaintiffs. The Court sets this matter for further hearing on March 4, 2022 at 9:00 am in Department 301 to confirm Defendants' compliance with this Order. If the Court finds that Defendants have not fully complied, the Court intends to issue terminating or issue preclusion sanctions. Counsel for Plaintiffs is directed to provide by February 28, 2022 a brief update via email on the status to Judge Pro Tem McDonald.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS TOM O'CONNOR, KEVIN COYLE, MICHAEL SCHWALM's MOTION To Compel For Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, To Produce Documents, Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts, And For An Award Of Monetary Sanctions is GRANTED IN PART and DENIED IN PART. Defendant must respond without objection to the four discovery requests at issue no later than September 10, 2021, absent a further extension agreed upon by the parties. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. The notice of motion does not "identify every person, party, and attorney against whom the sanction is sought and specify the type of sanction sought," as required by California Code of Civil Procedure section 2023.040.

Counsel for plaintiff to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email to mpritt@bsfllp.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Maxwell Pritt, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-09-2021 Continued To Sep-15-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-07-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: (Zoom)
David Monroe, 650.482.3040 for Plaintiff Tom O'Connor.
Jonathan Bornstein, 510.901.0010 for Defendant Laus Investment Group.

Argued by the Defendant. Jonathan Bornstein counsel for the Defendant argues re: the defined tern "Lau Defendants" to refer to alleged owners of the buildings.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS LAUS INVESTMENT GROUP, SOPHIE LAU, JEFFREY LAU and GINA LAU'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED as to all causes of action. All four of defendants' arguments made in support of their demurrer lack merit. There is nothing legally or factually problematic about using the defined term "Lau Defendants" to refer to alleged owners of the buildings. The individuals encompassed within that defined term are adequately alleged to be the owners because the entity defendant may not be legally distinct from the individuals and, in that event, the individuals are legally responsible for all contract and contract-like claims. The fraudulent concealment claim is pled with sufficient particularity. If defendants believe they need further detail, they may seek it via discovery. Plaintiffs adequately alleged extreme and outrageous conduct. Because all defendants filed an answer to the first amended complaint on May 11, 2021, no further response to the first amended complaint is needed. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)
